reagents.grade_purity: reactor-grade niobium ingot, ~99.9% (RRR > 100); 46.5 wt% Ti corresponds to ~53.5 wt% Nb
grade-1 titanium, ~99.95%; vacuum-arc-remelted (VAR) into NbTi ingot — multiple consumable-electrode remelts for homogeneity
OFHC oxygen-free high-conductivity copper, RRR>100, billet-machined with bored holes for NbTi rod inserts (mono-filament or multi-filament stack)
thin Nb sheet wrap around each NbTi filament, ~1-5 μm post-draw, prevents Cu-Ti intermetallic formation during heat treatment

conditions.atmosphere_gas: vacuum / inert (Ar) for hot extrusion; air for intermediate α-Ti precipitation anneals (Cu jacket is sealed)

provenance.source_citation: Bottura, L. & Godeke, A. 'Superconducting materials and conductors: fabrication and limiting parameters.' Annu. Rev. Mater. Res. 42, 25 (2012). Industrial NbTi process baseline since Berlincourt & Hake (1962) / Kunzler et al. discovery era; current vendors include Bruker EAS, Luvata, Furukawa, Western Superconducting Technologies (WST), supplying MRI / NMR / LHC dipole / ITER PF coils. LHC NbTi specification: CERN LHC Design Report Vol.1 Ch.7 (2004), cds.cern.ch/record/782076.

scope_caveats: Schema mapping note: the closest SynthesisRecipeRecord enum value is `pit_wire`, but NbTi is canonically a `stack & draw` (ductile-alloy-draw) process, not powder-in-tube. The actual process: VAR NbTi ingot → hot extrusion in Cu billet → cold drawing through reduction dies → multi-stage α-Ti precipitation anneals (~400°C, 24-40 h) → final wire diameter ~0.5-1.0 mm with filaments ~5-50 μm. `pit_wire` is used here as the schema's closest ductile-conductor route; RTSC.md §8.3 lists this under '합금 가공 (PIT wire)' as the umbrella label.

Temperature profile encodes two stages: (1) hot extrusion ~650°C (single representative step — actual extrusion is a single press, not isothermal hold), then (2) α-Ti precipitation anneal ~400°C × 40 h (typical 3-5 cycles interspersed with cold draw passes). The 49 h `duration_hours` is the cumulative anneal time only; total wire production (ingot → finished wire) is weeks to months and is not captured by this single-recipe envelope.

Composition Nb-46.5wt%Ti is the LHC / MRI workhorse; ITER PF uses similar; NMR uses higher-Ti variants. Jc(B,T) tuning is achieved by α-Ti precipitate size/density control via the anneal schedule, not by stoichiometry change.

replicated_by_independent_labs ≥ 8 reflects the industrial vendor set (Bruker, Luvata, Furukawa, WST, OST, SuperCon, Hitachi, Kiswire — non-exhaustive). Industry-standard since 1965. absorbed=false anyway because RTSC.md §8.7 Tier 2 ingest is recipe + citation only; Tier 3 measurement record (per-lot Jc(B,T) datasheet) and Tier 4 falsifier dispatch within demiurge remain ✗.
Vendor-spec ≠ lot-tested: each vendor's exact heat-treatment schedule and filament geometry are proprietary; this stub captures the publicly documented canonical route only.

Reagent mol_ratios are weight-fraction-derived molar ratios for the NbTi alloy itself; Cu matrix and Nb diffusion barrier carry mol_ratio=0.0 (they are geometric components, not part of the superconducting phase stoichiometry).
citations: Bottura, L. & Godeke, A. Annu. Rev. Mater. Res. 42, 25 (2012)
CERN LHC Design Report Vol.1 Ch.7 (2004) — cds.cern.ch/record/782076
Berlincourt, T. G. & Hake, R. R. Phys. Rev. 131, 140 (1963) — original NbTi Hc2 measurement
